Package org.apache.juneau.marshall.xml

Class XmlSerializerSession

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.marshall.ContextSession
org.apache.juneau.marshall.MarshallingSession
org.apache.juneau.marshall.MarshallingTraverseSession
org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.SerializerSession
org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.WriterSerializerSession
org.apache.juneau.marshall.xml.XmlSerializerSession
All Implemented Interfaces:
BeanSession, ConverterSession, RecordWritable
Direct Known Subclasses:
HtmlSerializerSession, SoapXmlSerializerSession, XmlDocSerializerSession
public class XmlSerializerSession extends WriterSerializerSession implements RecordWritable
Session object that lives for the duration of a single use of XmlSerializer.
Notes:
  • This class is not thread safe and is typically discarded after one use.
See Also:
  • XML Basics

  • Method Details

    • create

      public static XmlSerializerSession.Builder<?> create(XmlSerializer ctx)
      Creates a new builder for this object.
      Parameters:
      ctx - The context creating this session.
      Cannot be null.
      Returns:
      A new builder.

      public XmlBeanMeta getXmlBeanMeta(BeanMeta<?> bm)
      Returns the language-specific metadata on the specified bean.
      Parameters:
      bm - The bean to return the metadata on.
      Returns:
      The metadata.

      public XmlBeanPropertyMeta getXmlBeanPropertyMeta(BeanPropertyMeta bpm)
      Returns the language-specific metadata on the specified bean property.
      Parameters:
      bpm - The bean property to return the metadata on.
      Returns:
      The metadata.

      public XmlClassMeta getXmlClassMeta(ClassMeta<?> cm)
      Returns the language-specific metadata on the specified class.
      Parameters:
      cm - The class to return the metadata on.
      Returns:
      The metadata.

    • getXmlWriter

      public final XmlWriter getXmlWriter(SerializerPipe out)
      Converts the specified output target object to an XmlWriter.
      Parameters:
      out - The output target object.
      Returns:
      The output target object wrapped in an XmlWriter.

    • doSerialize

      protected void doSerialize(SerializerPipe out, Object o) throws IOException, SerializeException
      Description copied from class: SerializerSession
      Serializes a POJO to the specified pipe.

      This method should NOT close the context object.

      The default implementation of this method simply calls Serializer.doSerialize(SerializerSession,SerializerPipe,Object).

      Overrides:
      doSerialize in class SerializerSession
      Parameters:
      out - Where to send the output from the serializer.
      o - The object to serialize.
      Throws:
      IOException - Thrown by underlying stream.
      SerializeException - Problem occurred trying to serialize object.

    • serializeRecords

      public RecordWriter serializeRecords(Object output) throws IOException
      Opens a whole-value push generator targeting XML output, bound to this live session.

      Because XML's wire format (attributes, namespaces, mixed content) is non-trivial to expose as a fine-grained token writer, this implementation supports only the cursor-level POJO bridge — write(Object) delegates to SerializerSession.serialize(Object, Object).

      Specified by:
      serializeRecords in interface RecordWritable
      Parameters:
      output - The output.
      Returns:
      A new RecordWriter.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred opening the underlying output.

    • isRecordStreaming

      public boolean isRecordStreaming()
      The XML record writer is buffered/RecordAdapter-backed, not O(1) streaming.
      Specified by:
      isRecordStreaming in interface RecordWritable
      Returns:
      Always false.

    • findNsfMappings

      protected final void findNsfMappings(Object o) throws SerializeException
      Recursively searches for the XML namespaces on the specified POJO and adds them to the serializer context object.
      Parameters:
      o - The POJO to check.
      Throws:
      SerializeException - Thrown if bean recursion occurred.

    • getDefaultNamespace

      protected final Namespace getDefaultNamespace()
      Default namespace.
      Returns:
      The default namespace URI for this document.
      See Also:
      • XmlSerializer.Builder.defaultNamespace(Namespace)

    • getNamespaces

      protected final List<Namespace> getNamespaces()
      Default namespaces.
      Returns:
      The default list of namespaces associated with this serializer.
      Never null.
      List is modifiable (namespaces can be added during serialization).
      See Also:
      • XmlSerializer.Builder.namespaces(Namespace...)

    • isAddBeanTypes

      public boolean isAddBeanTypes()
      Description copied from class: SerializerSession
      Add "_type" properties when needed.
      Overrides:
      isAddBeanTypes in class SerializerSession
      Returns:
      true if "_type" properties added to beans if their type cannot be inferred through reflection.
      See Also:
      • Serializer.Builder.addBeanTypes()

    • isAddJsonTags

      protected final boolean isAddJsonTags()
      Add JSON type tags.
      Returns:
      true if plain strings will be wrapped in <string> tags when serialized as root elements.
      See Also:
      • XmlSerializer.Builder.disableJsonTags()

    • isAddNamespaceUrisToRoot

      protected final boolean isAddNamespaceUrisToRoot()
      Add namespace URLs to the root element.
      Returns:
      true if xmlns:x attributes are added to the root element for the default and all mapped namespaces.
      See Also:
      • XmlSerializer.Builder.addNamespaceUrisToRoot()

    • isAutoDetectNamespaces

      protected final boolean isAutoDetectNamespaces()
      Auto-detect namespace usage.
      Returns:
      true if namespace usage is detected before serialization.
      See Also:
      • XmlSerializer.Builder.disableAutoDetectNamespaces()

    • isEnableNamespaces

      protected final boolean isEnableNamespaces()
      Enable support for XML namespaces.
      Returns:
      false if XML output will not contain any namespaces regardless of any other settings.
      See Also:
      • XmlSerializer.Builder.enableNamespaces()

    • isHtmlMode

      protected boolean isHtmlMode()
      Returns true if we're serializing HTML.

      The difference in behavior is how empty non-void elements are handled. The XML serializer will produce a collapsed tag, whereas the HTML serializer will produce a start and end tag.

      Returns:
      true if we're generating HTML.

    • serializeAnything

      protected XmlSerializerSession.ContentResult serializeAnything(XmlWriter out, Object o, ClassMeta<?> eType, String keyName, String elementName, Namespace elementNamespace, boolean addNamespaceUris, XmlFormat format, boolean isMixedOrText, boolean preserveWhitespace, BeanPropertyMeta pMeta) throws SerializeException
      Workhorse method.
      Parameters:
      out - The writer to send the output to.
      o - The object to serialize.
      eType - The expected type if this is a bean property value being serialized.
      keyName - The property name or map key name.
      elementName - The root element name.
      elementNamespace - The namespace of the element.
      addNamespaceUris - Flag indicating that namespace URIs need to be added.
      format - The format to serialize the output to.
      isMixedOrText - We're serializing mixed content, so don't use whitespace.
      preserveWhitespace - true if we're serializing XmlFormat.MIXED_PWS or XmlFormat.TEXT_PWS.
      pMeta - The bean property metadata if this is a bean property being serialized.
      Returns:
      The same writer passed in so that calls to the writer can be chained.
      Throws:
      SerializeException - General serialization error occurred.
